from typing import List

@dataclass
class Token:

{"path":"tinylang/lexer.py","old_string":"   def read_punct(self) -> Token:
        """Read a punctuation token"""
        start_line = self.line

        # Check for multi-character operators first
        current_char = self.peek()
        if current_char in ['=', '!', '<', '>', '&', '|']:
            # Try to match multi-character operators
            two_char = current_char + self.peek(1)
            if two_char in ['==', '!=', '<=', '>=', '&&', '||']:
                self.advance()  # consume first char
                self.advance()  # consume second char

        # Single character punctuation
        punct = self.advance()
        return Token('PUNCT', punct, start_line, start_col)","new_string":"   def read_punct(self) -> Token:
        """Read a punctuation token"""
        start_line = self.line
        start_col = self.col
        
        # Check for multi-character operators first
        current_char = self.peek()
        if current_char in ['=', '!', '<', '>', '&', '|']:
            # Try to match multi-character operators
            two_char = current_char + self.peek(1)
            if two_char in ['==', '!=', '<=', '>=', '&&', '||']:
                self.advance()  # consume first char
                self.advance()  # consume second char
                return Token('PUNCT', two_char, start_line, start_col)
        
        # Single character punctuation

function.arguments:
{"path":"tinylang/lexer.py","old_string":"  def read_punct(self) -> Token:
        """Read a punctuation token"""
        start_line = self.line
        start_col = self.col
        
        # Check for multi-character operators first
        current_char = self.peek()
        if current_char in ['=', '!', '<', '>', '&', '|']:
            # Try to match multi-character operators
            two_char = current_char + self.peek(1)
            if two_char in ['==', '!=', '<=', '>=', '&&', '||']:
                self.advance()  # consume first char
                self.advance()  # consume second char
                return Token('PUNCT', two_char, start_line, start_col)
        
        # Single character punctuation
        punct = self.advance()
        return Token('PUNCT', punct, start_line, start_col)","new_string":"  def read_punct(self) -> Token:
        """Read a punctuation token"""
        start_line = self.line
        start_col = self.col
        
        # Check for multi-character operators first
        current_char = self.peek()
        if current_char in ['=', '!', '<', '>', '&', '|']:
            # Try to match multi-character operators
            if self.peek(1) is not None:
                two_char = current_char + self.peek(1)
                if two_char in ['==', '!=', '<=', '>=', '&&', '||']:
                    self.advance()  # consume first char
                    self.advance()  # consume second char
                    return Token('PUNCT', two_char, start_line, start_col)
        
        # Single character punctuation
        punct = self.advance()
        return Token('PUNCT', punct, start_line, start_col)"}
